title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer about forgiveness as a historical and social concept, examining instances where forgiveness functioned as a mechanism of control or social constraint rather than liberation. The conversation explores the intersection of history, philosophy, and human behavior.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer on forgiveness as a historical and social concept. The conversation centers on cases where forgiveness operated as a mechanism of control or social constraint rather than as a release. It draws on history, philosophy, and human behavior to examine how a practice often framed as liberating could instead function as a trap.
